staged_items.current_value: 9,629 worldwide (Coupe + Cab; Porsche Newsroom); ~4,130 North America
staged_items.proposed_change: Remove or flag ~4,130 North America as unverified
staged_items.rationale: The worldwide figure (9,629) is verified Tier 1. The ~4,130 North America sub-figure appears in no primary or secondary source found. At 43% of worldwide, this would be an unusually high NA share for a low-volume halo variant. Recommend either removing the NA sub-figure or annotating as '(unverified)' until a primary source is located.
staged_items.recommendation: Add '(unverified)' qualifier to NA figure, or remove NA sub-figure pending source

staged_items.rationale: No standalone Turbo (non-S) production figure found in any indexed source (Porsche Newsroom, Stuttcars, Wikipedia, Rennlist, Autoevolution). Stuttcars lists the MA1/71 engine code and specs but provides no production count. If Turbo S = 9,629 and the Turbo-to-Turbo S ratio is ~1:2 (Rennlist community consensus), then Turbo ≈ 4,800. The ~5,350 figure is plausible but unverified.
staged_items.recommendation: Add '(unverified)' qualifier or remove until primary source confirmed

staged_items.rationale: Multiple secondary sources cluster at ~6,300 worldwide: Stuttcars cites '~6,300 units', porscheknowledge.com cites '6,300 units for 991.1 GT3'. A Rennlist community thread cites 6,520 with a per-year NA breakdown: 2014=602, 2015=1,342, 2016=189 totaling 2,133. The current ~6,050 is slightly low vs the consensus. Neither figure is from a Porsche primary source.

sources.name: Rennlist unofficial GT3 RS stats thread

staged_items.recommendation: Update to ~6,300 worldwide — more consistent with sources. NA ~2,133 is a modest uplift from ~2,070 and supported by Rennlist per-year breakdown.

staged_items.rationale: Multiple secondary sources cluster at 4,520–4,596 worldwide: nweuro.com cites '4,596 worldwide | USA 1,318 | Canada 211 = 1,529 NA'; autofolio.info cites '4,596 globally'; porscheknowledge.com cites '4,500'; Rennlist unofficial cites '4,520 worldwide / 1,530 NA'. The current ~4,250 is consistently below all available source figures. NA figure ~1,530 is well-supported and accurate as-is.
staged_items.recommendation: Update to ~4,596 worldwide; NA stays ~1,529–1,530 (consistent, accurate)

staged_items.rationale: 1,963 worldwide is verified by Porsche Newsroom and Wikipedia. The ~750 North America sub-figure appears in no primary or secondary source found. Based on Porsche's typical NA allocation for special editions (~35–40% of worldwide), a plausible estimate would be ~690–785. The ~750 figure may be correct but is not sourced.

staged_items.field_path: engineering[GT3 rod-bolt recall].body + watch_for[GT3 rod-bolt recall].body
staged_items.current_value: approximately 785 cars worldwide (~400 US) had their complete engines replaced
staged_items.proposed_change: approximately 785 US vehicles covered by the recall had their engines replaced
staged_items.rationale: NHTSA recall 14V-090 document explicitly states 785 vehicles affected in the US (manufactured Oct 19, 2013 – Jan 16, 2014). The figure 785 is the US count, not a worldwide count. The current text ('785 worldwide, ~400 US') appears to have the relationship inverted. The worldwide recall count (including European/global markets) is not stated in NHTSA documentation and would likely be higher than 785. The '~400 US' sub-figure may refer to cars that had been delivered to customers vs those still in dealer stock at the time of the recall.

sources.name: Wikipedia 911 GT3
sources.quote: 'all 785 GT3s produced up to that point were recalled'

staged_items.note: Affects two locations: engineering[] array and watch_for[] array. Both currently say '785 cars worldwide (~400 US)'. Correction direction: 785 is the US/NHTSA-scope figure. Worldwide total is unknown but higher.
staged_items.recommendation: Change to: 'approximately 785 US vehicles had their engines replaced (NHTSA recall 14V-090); worldwide total is undisclosed by Porsche but higher'

staged_items.rationale: The 991 Carrera 4 and Carrera 4S were announced at the 2012 Paris Motor Show as MY2013 models. The base Carrera and Carrera S launched as MY2012. Classic.com market data confirms production years 2013–2016 for both Carrera 4 and Carrera 4S. Secondary source confidence; not verified from a Porsche primary press release.
staged_items.recommendation: Update Carrera 4 and Carrera 4S years to '2013–2016' after confirming via Porsche Newsroom/press kit

staged_items.proposed_change: Review whether 8,634 total includes Carrera 4 GTS (AWD) in the Coupe/Cab split
staged_items.rationale: The label says 'RWD GTS' but the breakdown (5,510 Coupe + 3,124 Cab = 8,634) may represent all Carrera GTS body types (coupe + cab) including both RWD and AWD (Carrera 4 GTS). The split between RWD and AWD within the GTS totals is not available from public sources. If the 8,634 figure includes Carrera 4 GTS coupes, calling it 'RWD GTS' is misleading. However, this cannot be confirmed without a primary source breakdown.
staged_items.recommendation: Either (a) verify the 8,634 is RWD-only (Coupe + Cab) and retain the label, or (b) update to 'all Carrera GTS variants (Coupe + Cab; RWD + AWD)'. Pending source confirmation.

staged_items.current_value: 3.4L 9A1 NA (Carrera/C4: 350 hp); 3.8L 9A1 NA (Carrera S/4S/GTS: 400–430 hp); 3.8L 9A1 twin-turbo (Turbo: 520 hp; Turbo S: 560 hp)
staged_items.proposed_change: Notation clarification only — '9A1' is correct as a colloquial family name but the Turbo/Turbo S specific code is MA1/71
staged_items.rationale: Research confirms: '9A1' is the commonly used colloquial family name for all modern water-cooled Porsche flat-sixes, but technically the Turbo/Turbo S engine is sub-designated MA1/71 (per Stuttcars). The NA Carrera engines are MA1/MAB variants. MA175 = GT3 3.8L; MA176 = GT3 RS 4.0L. The current usage of '9A1' for the entire family is standard industry shorthand and is not wrong — this is a precision note only.
staged_items.recommendation: No change required — '9A1' is accepted industry shorthand. Optional: add '(MA1/71)' parenthetical to the Turbo engine entry for precision. Not urgent.

staged_items.rationale: 991 worldwide is universally confirmed (symbolic production limit). ~323 North America is sourced from the Rennlist unofficial production stats thread, not a Porsche primary source. One secondary source cites 325 NA. The ~323 figure is plausible and consistent across community sources but not Porsche-primary verified.

sources.name: Rennlist unofficial GT3 RS stats thread

staged_items.recommendation: Retain as-is with awareness that ~323 NA is community-sourced. May add '(unofficial)' qualifier.
